Grit,
Growth,
and Grace.

Psychology senior. HR practitioner. Project manager. Violinist. I study people for a living — then build things that make their lives a little less chaotic.

The person behind the paperwork

About
Yshi

I’m a senior Psychology student at De La Salle University and a DOST Merit Scholar — which means yes, I earned my spot, and yes, the pressure is real.

I’ve spent the last few years doing what I love most: understanding people, then building systems to support them. I’ve recruited and onboarded at ING Hubs, run workshops for kids across Metro Manila, directed HR & PM operations at SCOOP, and advocated for mental health at MHTF.

I’m also a violinist, conductor, and violin tutor who has been leading rehearsals since 2016 — which taught me that the best kind of leadership is the kind that listens first.

I don’t just collect experience. I collect the right tools, then I use them with intention.

Why Yshi

Three words that
actually mean something

G
Grit
Hard-working · Strategic · Innovative
I don’t just work hard — I work with direction. Whether it’s screening 1,000+ LinkedIn profiles as a recruitment intern, spearheading 10+ events as HRPM Director, or winning a case study competition on AI ethics, I bring energy and strategy to everything I touch. I find ways where others find walls.
“I don’t just show up. I show up prepared, with a plan, and ready to iterate.”
G
Growth
Deliberate · Curious · Expert-in-the-making
I don’t wait for growth to happen to me — I go looking for it. From Google PM certifications to Wharton marketing courses to music pedagogy training, I deliberately build the toolkit I need to do my field justice. I study what I want to master. That’s not ambition. That’s accountability to the work.
“I collect the right tools, then I use them with intention.”
G
Grace
Human · Accountable · Heart-led
I know I’m not perfect — and I’ve made peace with that. Grace means I show up honestly, own my mistakes, and keep my heart in the work. I became a Psychological First Aid trainee, a Red Cross First Aider, and a mental health advocate because I genuinely believe people deserve to be understood.
“I’m not here to be flawless. I’m here to be real, and to make it better.”
